articles.title: Multimodal RAG with the Gemini API File Search Tool: A Developer Guide
articles.description: The File Search tool in the Gemini API now supports multimodal retrieval by adding support for Gemini Embedding 2 . This update allows images, such as charts, product photos, and diagrams, to be natively indexed and searched in the same store as your text-based documents. This post covers how to use
articles.content: The File Search tool in the Gemini API now supports multimodal retrieval by adding support for Gemini Embedding 2 . This update allows images, such as charts, product photos, and diagrams, to be natively indexed and searched in the same store as your text-based documents. This post covers how to use the File Search tool end-to-end: creating a store, uploading documents and images, querying with grounded generation, and retrieving image citations. What is File Search? Here's an example app you ca
articles.author: Patrick Loeber

articles.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
articles.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits
articles.content: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT . In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. Elon Musk took the stand on April 28th as the first witness called, portraying his interest in founding OpenAI as an effort to help save humanity across three days of testimony, before his f
articles.author: Elizabeth Lopatto

articles.title: Meta sued by major book publishers over copyright infringement
articles.description: Meta is facing a class action lawsuit filed by five major book publishers and one author over claims the company "engaged in one of the most massive infringements of copyrighted materials in history" when training its Llama AI models, as reported earlier by The New York Times. In their suit, Macmill
articles.content: Meta is facing a class action lawsuit filed by five major book publishers and one author over claims the company "engaged in one of the most massive infringements of copyrighted materials in history" when training its Llama AI models, as reported earlier by The New York Times . In their suit , Macmillan, McGraw Hill, Elsevier, Hachette, Cengage, and author Scott Turow allege that Meta "repeatedly copied" their books and journal articles without permission. The lawsuit accuses Meta of knowingly r
articles.author: Emma Roth

articles.description: According to Pennsylvania's filing, a Character AI chatbot presented itself as a licensed psychiatrist during a state investigation, and also fabricated a serial number for its state medical license.

articles.title: Building a Zulip Style Collaborative Chat App with Next.js and Velt
articles.description: Zulip is known for keeping conversations organized. Topic-based threads, clear context, and async-friendly discussions make it a favorite for technical and distributed teams. Unlike traditional chat apps, conversations in Zulip stay readable even as teams scale. Recreating this experience can be a b
articles.content: Zulip is known for keeping conversations organized. Topic-based threads, clear context, and async-friendly discussions make it a favorite for technical and distributed teams. Unlike traditional chat apps, conversations in Zulip stay readable even as teams scale. Recreating this experience can be a bit difficult. Real-time messaging, user presence, inline comments, and notifications usually require a complex backend and real-time infrastructure. In this tutorial, we will build a Zulip-style colla
articles.author: Astrodevil

articles.title: OpenAI claims ChatGPT’s new default model hallucinates way less
articles.description: OpenAI's newest default model for ChatGPT might not make stuff up as much. Hallucinations have been an ongoing problem for AI models, but OpenAI says its new GPT-5.5 Instant model has "significant improvements in factuality across the board." The company claims that, based on "internal evaluations,"
articles.content: OpenAI's newest default model for ChatGPT might not make stuff up as much. Hallucinations have been an ongoing problem for AI models, but OpenAI says its new GPT-5.5 Instant model has "significant improvements in factuality across the board." The company claims that, based on "internal evaluations," GPT-5.5 Instant produced "52.5% fewer hallucinated claims" than its Instant model for GPT-5.3 "on high-stakes prompts covering areas like medicine, law, and finance." GPT-5.5 Instant also "reduced in
articles.author: Jay Peters

articles.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 Instant, a new default model for ChatGPT
articles.description: The company said the model reduces hallucination in sensitive areas such as law, medicine, and finance, while maintaining the low latency of its predecessor.
articles.content: The company said the model reduces hallucination in sensitive areas such as law, medicine, and finance, while maintaining the low latency of its predecessor.

After focusing on highly requested Xbox console features, reducing the price of Game Pass, and moving Microsoft Gaming back to Xbox, Sharma is now reorganizing the Xbox platform tea
articles.content: Microsoft's new Xbox chief, Asha Sharma, has spent the past couple of months making her mark on the Xbox organization. After focusing on highly requested Xbox console features, reducing the price of Game Pass , and moving Microsoft Gaming back to Xbox , Sharma is now reorganizing the Xbox platform team at Microsoft today. The changes will see some veterans depart, promotions, and new faces with more technical expertise to help the Xbox platform team. Jared Palmer, who used to work with Sharma in
articles.author: Tom Warren

articles.title: Register now for OpenClaw: After Hours @ GitHub
articles.description: OpenClaw builders will gather at GitHub HQ during Microsoft Build 2026 for demos and conversations. Join in person, or watch the livestream on Twitch. The post Register now for OpenClaw: After Hours @ GitHub appeared first on The GitHub Blog .
articles.content: OpenClaw , one of the fastest-growing open source projects, has already picked up over 350,000 stars and an early community of builders exploring what agentic systems can actually do in practice. That’s why, on June 3, 2026, we are hosting OpenClaw: After Hours at GitHub HQ in San Francisco. The event will take place during Microsoft Build 2026 . This evening is a chance to bring the OpenClaw community together into the same room. We’ll kick things off in the early evening with a fir
articles.author: Lee Reilly

articles.description: DHS targeted a man who hadn't entered the US for years for criticism of ICE operations.
articles.content: The Department of Homeland Security tried to obtain a Canadian man’s location information, activity logs, and other identifying information from Google after he criticized the Trump administration online following the killings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti by federal immigration agents in Minneapolis early this year. Lawyers for the man, who has not been named, are alarmed in part because they say that the man has not entered the United States in more than a decade. “I don’t know what the govern
articles.author: Maddy Varner, wired.com.

articles.title: How do you design a $30,000 electric pickup? Inside Ford's skunkworks.
articles.description: We tour Ford’s top-secret Electric Vehicle Development Center in California.
articles.content: LONG BEACH, Calif.—2026 is a strange time for electric vehicles in the US. The current administration has no desire to push for their adoption and has rescinded the federal tax credit on which EV sales have depended for years. Tariffs have made vehicles and their constituent components even more expensive , making switching to an EV for the first time an even harder pill to swallow. Manufacturers like Honda, which had three nearly production-ready EVs on deck, just killed them all unceremoniousl
articles.author: Kyle Hyatt

articles.title: What (un)exactly do you mean by semantic search?
articles.description: Ryan welcomes Bryan O’Grady, Head of Field Research and Solutions Architecture at Qdrant, to discuss the differences between traditional text search engines powered by Lucene and modern vector databases, when vector search’s exact-match needs work for things like logs and security analytics and when
articles.content: Ryan welcomes Bryan O’Grady, Head of Field Research and Solutions Architecture at Qdrant, to discuss the differences between traditional text search engines powered by Lucene and modern vector databases, when vector search’s exact-match needs work for things like logs and security analytics and when semantic search works for user-facing discovery and non-exact results, and how Qdrant is growing into video embeddings and local-agent contexts.
articles.author: Phoebe Sajor

articles.title: A blueprint for using AI to strengthen democracy
articles.description: Every few centuries, changes in how information moves reshape how societies govern themselves. The printing press spread vernacular literacy, helping give rise to the Reformation and, eventually, representative government. The telegraph made it possible to administer vast nations like the US, accele
articles.content: Every few centuries, changes in how information moves reshape how societies govern themselves. The printing press spread vernacular literacy, helping give rise to the Reformation and, eventually, representative government. The telegraph made it possible to administer vast nations like the US, accelerating the growth of the modern bureaucratic state. Broadcast media created shared national audiences, which in turn fueled mass democracy. We are now in the early stages of another such shift. Faster
articles.author: Andrew Sorota, Josh Hendler

articles.title: Welcome to Maintainer Month: Celebrating the people behind the code
articles.description: What maintainers are telling us, what we've shipped, and how to celebrate the people behind open source. The post Welcome to Maintainer Month: Celebrating the people behind the code appeared first on The GitHub Blog .
articles.content: At a Maintainer Unconference in Brussels this year, a breakout session had maintainers jotting down thoughts on the future of open source. One sticky note stood out: As AI gets better at writing code, human work around code becomes more important and more invisible. Mentoring new contributors, building trust across a community, making the judgement calls that shape a project’s direction: that’s the work that turns a repository into a living collaboration. And with the speed of AI, th
articles.author: Abigail Cabunoc Mayes

articles.title: Charlize Theron is a bewitching Circe in Odyssey trailer
articles.description: "You're a man who needs to control his fate. But you cannot control this."
articles.content: Over the last year, Christopher Nolan and Universal Pictures have been trickling out sneak peeks and teasers for Nolan's forthcoming film version of The Odyssey , adapting Homer's classic poem. The studio recently dropped a new two-and-a-half-minute trailer stuffed with the glorious visuals, high emotions, and soaring music befitting such an epic saga. As previously reported , most of us read some version of The Odyssey in high school, so we’re familiar with the story: Odysseus, legendary Greek

articles.title: Week one of the Musk v. Altman trial: What it was like in the room
articles.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Two of the most powerful people in AI—Sam Altman and Elon Musk—began their face-off in court in Oakland, California, last week. Musk is suing OpenAI, allegin
articles.content: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here . Two of the most powerful people in AI—Sam Altman and Elon Musk—began their face-off in court in Oakland, California, last week. Musk is suing OpenAI, alleging that the millions he spent to fund it around a decade ago were meant for a nonprofit, not a corporation, and that the company has reneged on that mission since. The stakes are high—even a partial win fo
articles.author: James O'Donnell

articles.title: Beyond the Code: Mentoring the Next Generation of Engineers at Six Flags
articles.description: Technology is often viewed through the lens of lines of code and GitHub commits, but its true impact lies in the community it builds. Recently, representatives from Open Source with SLU stepped away from our development environments to serve as ambassadors for the future of computer science during a
articles.content: Technology is often viewed through the lens of lines of code and GitHub commits, but its true impact lies in the community it builds. Recently, representatives from Open Source with SLU stepped away from our development environments to serve as ambassadors for the future of computer science during a regional outreach event. While the setting was informal, the goal was high-stakes: inspiring curiosity in middle and high school students. As a representative of our open-source program, my role wasn

articles.title: Musk's Europe gamble: Will others follow the Dutch and approve FSD?
articles.description: The Dutch road authority will ask other EU regulators to approve the driver assist.
articles.content: Following last year's Tesla shareholder vote, CEO Elon Musk's near-incomprehensible wealth is now inextricably linked in part to the number of active "FSD" subscriptions his electric car company can sign up. And last month, the Dutch vehicle regulator RDW made that a little easier by approving FSD for use on its roads. Now, the RDW will ask the rest of the European Union to follow suit, opening up a new market of 450 million potential new customers for the driver assist. But it's no foregone con
articles.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
